texto: I like being an exhibitionist with you. Besides, you can't say we don't have the climate for it.” “I don't know, I'd say there's the potential for sunburn in truly terrible places.” “This is true,” Jay said gravely. “In all cases of uncontrollable lust, safety first. Seek shade, apply sunscreen, and only then can you fornicate wildly.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Libby Cole: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I like being an exhibitionist with you. Besides, you can't say we don't have the climate for it.”…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, humour, romance pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de romance.

frase_original: I like being an exhibitionist with you. Besides, you can't say we don't have the climate for it.” “I don't know, I'd say there's the potential for sunburn in truly terrible places.” “This is true,” Jay said gravely. “In all cases of uncontrollable lust, safety first. Seek shade, apply sunscreen, and only then can you fornicate wildly.

texto: Jay bent over the table, and the muscles in his arm stood proud as he drew the cue back. In one swift motion, he sent it cracking into the white, the other balls soon spinning across the green felt.“Your turn,” he handed her the cue, eyes glinting wickedly. “Need me to look after your end? Of the pool cue, I mean.” “I'm quite capable of looking after my own end, thanks,” Kayla replied archly, and quickly ran the chalk over her cue. “I know the importance of taking care of the tip.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Libby Cole que provoca temas ligados a amor. Ao questionar “Jay bent over the table, and the muscles in his arm stood proud as he drew the cue back. In one s…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, funny-quotes, romance, com foco em termino e romance.
